finding_results.notes: Recast the flagged clipped narration beats into complete sentences and smoothed nearby fragment carryover in the bat-netting and breakfast sections.

Compressed the surveillance and power-asymmetry explanation into one warning, then attached the tomorrow-night invitation to concrete terms: Mina must report to the lab at dusk because Rhea asked, stop using the survey as cover, and leave the bedrooms out of it.

summary: Chapter 4 now removes the packet's clipped narration beats and rebuilds the breakfast confrontation so Rhea states the camp-risk once, draws a practical line around tomorrow night, and makes Mina accept that cost on the page.
